lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: So they're from the west?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Yes! They call it Varlamore, the Shining Kingdom! It's meant to be one of the most beautiful places in the world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Oh, nothing you need to worry about.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Oh, hello! Are you here to see the delegation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Delegation?

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: See! It's not just me who doesn't know what's happening.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: The delegation from Varlamore, of course! After all these years, they're finally opening up the kingdom for travel and trade! Isn't it exciting?

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: Why would I be excited about a place I never even knew existed?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: A whole land locked off for hundreds of years! Do you not want to be one of the first outsiders to step foot there? Think of the adventure!

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: Adventure? Sounds dangerous. I'll be staying right here, thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Bah! Boring! What about you, stranger?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm actually something of an adventurer myself.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: That's the spirit! I bet you'll want to be one of the first to head over to Varlamore!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: It's far across the western sea, over the mountains south of Great Kourend. Between the sea and the mountains, it's totally isolated! No one is allowed to come or go without good reason.

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: Sounds like a prison.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Quite the opposite! It's a beautiful and prosperous kingdom, ruled over by the Sun Queen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Sun Queen?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Yes! She rules from her palace in their capital, Civitas illa Fortis. The sun plays an important role in Varlamorian life. In fact, they worship it!

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: What... the sun?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: That's right! They have their own religion over there dedicated to the sun and the moon. Isn't that fascinating?

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: I don't know... It all sounds a bit odd to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come you know so much about Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: A while back, a group of travellers came through here, on their way to the dark lands of Morytania. They said they were from the College of Bards!

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: College of Bards?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Yes! It's found in the floating city of Tempestus! Members of the College are some of the only people allowed to travel to and from Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: They stayed the night in the Blue Moon Inn and spent hours telling us all about their homeland. I've been dying to visit ever since!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When will this delegation arrive?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Well it should be any time now!

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: Hang on... what's going on over there?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Wait! That's them! They're here!

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: There they are: The children of the sun! That's Itzla Arkan, heir to the throne of Varlamore! And there's Servius, the Teokan of Ralos!

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: Teokan? What's a Teokan?

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: It means High Priest. He's the religious leader in Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: Huh...

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: What are you doing with that bag?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh... Er... Just delivering some supplies to the guards at the gates.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Well hurry back. We need everyone at their posts.

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: That's an unusually big bag he's carrying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Now where might you be going with that...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I need to head off.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure. Doesn't really sound like my kind of thing.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Well... suit yourself, I guess.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: A whole land locked off for hundreds of years! Do you not want to be one of the first outsiders to step foot there? Think of the adventure!

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: Adventure? Sounds dangerous. I'll be staying right here, thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Bah! Boring! What about you, stranger?

lines.speaker: [Noah/Alina]
lines.text: Sorry, I can't stop to talk right now.

lines.speaker: Alina
lines.text: Hello again! So what do you think? Reckon you'll be one of the first to head over to Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! What are you doing?

sections.title: Talking to Noah or Alina after starting to tail the guard

lines.speaker: Noah
lines.text: That was quite a large bag that guard was carrying. I wonder where he's taking it...

sections.title: Talking to Sergeant Tobyn after starting to tail the guard

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Move along, citizen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you up to in there...?

lines.speaker: Bandit (red hood)
lines.text: You're late.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm here aren't I? If you're not happy, maybe you should have been the one to steal the uniforms!

lines.speaker: Bandit (woman)
lines.text: Enough! We don't have much time. Do you have everything?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Mostly.

lines.speaker: Bandit (red hood)
lines.text: Mostly?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I grabbed what I could!

lines.speaker: Bandit (bearded)
lines.text: We're just going to have to make do. The delegation is already in the palace. We need to be ready to strike as they leave.

lines.speaker: Bandit (tanned)
lines.text: Indeed. You all know the target. Get changed quick and be ready to take your positions.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Good luck. You're going to need it.

lines.speaker: Bandit (bearded)
lines.text: You lot panic too much. It's going to be fine. Now, let's get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This doesn't sound good. I'd better let that sergeant know.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Get out there and point out the four bandits. They're in disguise, so be sure to keep a close eye out for any guard who looks or acts differently to the others.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Remember, if they intend to attack the delegation, they'll either be here in the square or on the eastern road between the square and the bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Move along, citizen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! I have some information that you need to hear.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are some bandits disguising themselves as guards! I think they're planning to attack the delegation from Varlamore!

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Surely you can't be serious?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's true! One of them infiltrated your guards and stole some uniforms. He came right through here earlier with them.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: The one with the large bag? I thought I didn't recognise him, but then it's so hard to keep up. We get through guards so quickly around here for some reason...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we do? Should we warn the delegation?

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: And risk a panic? No. Better we deal with this now while they're safe in the palace. Do you know how many bandits there are?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Four in total.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Right, we can work with that. You get out there and point them out. With them being disguised, you'll need to keep a close eye out for any guard who looks or acts differently to the others.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Don't go too far. If the bandits intend to attack the delegation, they'll either be here in the square or on the eastern road between the square and the bank.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Return to me once you've found all four and we'll take them all at the same time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll get to it.

sections.title: Talking to Sergeant Tobyn after marking the imposters

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I've pointed out all the bandits.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Are you sure? I definitely recognise at least one of them as one of my guards. Maybe you should try again.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Good work. I'll signal my guards to bring them in...

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: That could have ended up quite nasty if not for you. Once again, good work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happens now?

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: The bandits will be interrogated. If all goes well, we'll get some useful information out of them.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Speaking of which... given it was you who found them in the first place, you might be able to offer some support. Would you be willing to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! Where do you need me?

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Come with me. We'll use the cell on the palace roof for the interrogations. It's nice and out of the way.

sections.title: Talking to Sergeant Tobyn after turning in the guards

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: That could have ended up quite nasty if not for you. Once again, good work.

sections.title: Talking to the Prince or Sergeant

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, nilsal, sergeant.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Prince Itzla! I... I'm sorry, but I wasn't expecting you here.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well all that politics downstairs was getting a bit dry, but then I heard there'd been a bit of a commotion. I thought I'd come see what the fuss was all about. You don't mind, do you?

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Well no, it's just...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Wonderful! And you! Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: [player name]? Kuaini! Pleased to meet you. Crown Prince Itzla Arkan, heir to the Sun Throne, at your service.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Tell me, [player name], are you the one who caught these would-be assassins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well then, why don't you and I ask this one a few little questions. You don't mind, do you, sergeant?

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Not at all, but...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Excellent! Let's get started then.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal to you, iknami. I hear you managed to get yourself into a spot of bother.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: I'm not telling you anything.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, tetamo! Such a shame. No matter though! We'll have you talking in no time.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Now, you'll need to forgive me, but I'm a bit new around here, so your customs are not familiar to me. Tell me, iknami, what's the preferred method of interrogation in these parts?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: You're asking me how you should interrogate me?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well it just seemed polite to ask. We have all sorts of fun methods back home in Varlamore. We could give one or two of them a go if you want?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: If I want?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Absolutely! Now, what are your thoughts on chicken?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: Chicken?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Oh, [player name], do you not have chickens here? Sorry, again, all this is quite new to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, we have chickens. The farms around here have loads.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well then, why the confusion? Have you never seen one before? Oh you poor sheltered thing. Did your parents never show you the wonders of the countryside?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: I know what a chicken is!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well why didn't you say so?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: I don't know what game you're playing, but it won't work on me!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: No game at all, iknami. The reason I ask is because one of our preferred methods in Varlamore involves just a small amount of fire, and you'd never believe it, but the smell of human flesh once it gets going...

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: You're going to cook me?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Cook? No, not at all! That would imply I'm going to eat you, and I'm not a barbarian! Though, it would be interesting to know if it tastes like chicken as well...

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: Alright, enough! I'll tell you what you want!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Kuaini!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: So who are you working for?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: I don't know their name. They just paid us to attack the delegation. They didn't look to be from around here. I reckon it was one of your lot!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Why did they want you to attack the delegation?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: They didn't say, but that priest... he was the target!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: So a Varlamorian paid you to assassinate the Teokan? You're not giving me much to go on.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: That's all I know!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well I think we're done here. Come, [player name].

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I think we got what we needed there.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: You're sure he told you everything?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Oh yes. The fear of being cooked will do that to a person. All total rubbish of course! Imagine burning someone alive! We're much more civilised over in Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We just cut out their heart while it's still beating instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't tell if you're joking or not...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Anyway, sounds like there'll be some work to do when we get back home. An assassination attempt on the Teokan of Ralos... Concerning stuff. First up though, I'd better get back to the politics!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: [player name], once we've got these papers signed, you lot will be free to travel to Varlamore for the first time! No more needing explicit permission. You can come and go as you please!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Now, it's entirely up to you of course, but given we have you to thank for stopping this plot, I'd be very keen if you'd join me in Varlamore to continue the investigation.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: No need to decide now. It will take a bit longer for us to be ready for visitors anyway, but when the time comes, I'll have my people send word to you.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: No need to decide now, but if you do wish to accept my offer, just speak to Regulus Cento outside the eastern gates of this city.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Now, they'll be missing me downstairs. Timoiva, and may the sun light your way!

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Well, that was interesting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not every day the heir to a far-off land comes and interrogates one of your prisoners?

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Oddly enough, no. This will be an interesting report to write. And on that note, I'd best get to it. All the best, adventurer. Thank you for your help.

sections.title: Talking again after starting the interrogation cutscene
Talking again after finishing the interrogation

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I think we got what we needed there.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Tobyn
lines.text: Well, that was interesting...
